content.question: What magnitude of within-country heterogeneity in track effects exists in Hungary? Do tracks benefit only elite students in advantaged schools, or do they also provide value for high-achieving students from disadvantaged backgrounds, and does this heterogeneity vary by region/urbanization?
content.reduction_path: Stratified analysis of Horn's panel data (or equivalent) by student baseline SES, prior achievement, and school location; separate estimation of track effects for top-quartile low-SES students versus top-quartile high-SES students; regional comparison of track effects in Budapest versus smaller cities versus rural areas.
